subject: Social Science
title: Tracing Changes Through a Thousand Years

questions.text: What does the term 'Hindustan' signify today according to the text?
questions.explanation: In the 13th century, it meant areas around Punjab and Haryana; today, we use it to mean the modern nation-state of India.

questions.text: Cartography is the science of making:
questions.explanation: A person who makes maps is called a cartographer.

questions.text: In the medieval period, a 'foreigner' was someone who:
options: Was not part of a particular society or culture
questions.explanation: Any stranger who appeared in a given village, who was not a part of that society, was considered a foreigner (pardesi).
Manuscripts were expensive and were kept in libraries and archives by wealthy patrons and institutions.

questions.text: The place where documents and manuscripts are stored is called:
questions.explanation: Archives are repositories for historical records and documents.

questions.text: Who was a scribe in the medieval period?
questions.explanation: Scribes copied manuscripts by hand before the invention of the printing press.

questions.text: The term 'Jati' refers to groups ranked on the basis of their:
questions.explanation: Jatis or sub-castes were ranked based on their background and their occupations.

questions.text: Which new crop was introduced to the subcontinent during this period?
questions.explanation: New foods and beverages like potatoes, corn, chillies, tea, and coffee arrived in the subcontinent.

questions.text: Which group became important in this period and claimed Rajput status?
questions.explanation: Groups like Marathas, Sikhs, Jats, Ahoms, and Kayasthas used the opportunities of the age to become politically important.

questions.text: The 14th-century chronicler who wrote his chronicle in two versions was:
questions.explanation: Ziyauddin Barani wrote his chronicle first in 1356 and another version two years later.
